 {"id":"gt-4u5z","title":"Refinery as Worktree: Local MR Integration","description":"Move refinery from separate clone to git worktree. Polecats stop pushing to origin - refinery sees their branches locally. MRs become wisps (ephemeral). Only main gets pushed after merge.

## Goals
- Origin stays clean (only main + beads-sync branches)
- No orphaned polecat branches ever
- Simpler mental model for MR coordination
- Faster integration (no network for local MR ops)

## Trade-offs
- Machine crash = redo pending work (acceptable, beads track state)
- Future federation needs different approach (bundles or integration branch)

## Components Affected
- Rig initialization (refinery setup)
- Spawn/polecat workflow (remove origin push)
- Refinery manager (local branch access)
- Documentation (architecture, workflows)
- Molecule templates (remove push instructions)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:24:56.517669-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:28:22.512455-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T21:28:22.512455-08:00","close_reason":"Complete: refinery as worktree, local branches, wisp MRs"}
 {"id":"gt-4u5z.1","title":"Update rig init to create refinery as worktree","description":"Modify rig initialization to create refinery as a git worktree instead of a separate clone.

## Changes
- internal/rig/init.go or similar - change refinery setup
- Create worktree at <rig>/refinery/ with branch 'refinery'
- Remove clone logic for refinery
- Update any path assumptions

## Considerations
- Refinery worktree should track main (or its own branch?)
- May need to handle existing rigs (migration path)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:25.927013-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:31:07.100875-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T20:31:07.100875-08:00","close_reason":"Rig init now creates refinery as worktree of mayor clone","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.1","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:25.92745-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4u5z.2","title":"Remove origin push from polecat workflow","description":"Polecats should no longer push their branches to origin.

## Changes
- internal/cmd/done.go - remove 'git push origin HEAD'
- internal/cmd/mq_submit.go - remove branch push
- Molecule templates - remove push instructions
- Any hooks that push polecat branches

## Verification
- Polecat completes work without pushing
- Branch stays local
- Refinery can still see the branch (via shared .git)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:27.079456-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:33:43.173059-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T20:33:43.173059-08:00","close_reason":"Removed origin push from done.go, mq_submit.go, spawn.go, and molecule templates","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:27.081989-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.547484-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

+{"id":"gt-gpgdv","title":"Optimize: Share stepMap across control flow operators","description":"In ApplyControlFlow, both ApplyBranches and ApplyGates call buildStepMap() separately:

```go
func ApplyBranches(steps []*Step, compose *ComposeRules) ([]*Step, error) {
    stepMap := buildStepMap(steps)  // Called here
    ...
}

func ApplyGates(steps []*Step, compose *ComposeRules) ([]*Step, error) {
    stepMap := buildStepMap(steps)  // Called again here
    ...
}
```

This is inefficient for large formulas. Options:

1. Pass stepMap as a parameter to ApplyBranches/ApplyGates
2. Create an internal version that accepts stepMap, keep public API unchanged
3. Build once in ApplyControlFlow and pass down

Low priority since formula sizes are typically small.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:14:10.961538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T15:14:10.961538-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gpgdv","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:14:19.033379-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gswn","title":"Integration test: agent waits for CI via gate","description":"End-to-end test of the gate workflow.

## Test Scenario
1. Agent creates gate: bd gate create --await gh:run:123 --timeout 5m --notify beads/dave
2. Agent writes handoff and exits
3. Deacon patrol checks gate condition
4. (Mock) GitHub run completes
5. Deacon notifies waiter and closes gate
6. New agent session reads mail and resumes

## Test Requirements
- Mock GitHub API responses
- Test timeout path
- Test multiple waiters
- Verify mail notifications sent

 {"id":"gt-ng6g","title":"Implement await type handlers (gh:run, gh:pr, timer, human, mail)","description":"Implement condition checking for each await type.

## Handlers Needed
- gh:run:<id> - Check GitHub Actions run status via gh CLI
- gh:pr:<id> - Check PR merged/closed status via gh CLI  
- timer:<duration> - Simple elapsed time check
- human:<prompt> - Check for human approval (via mail?)
- mail:<pattern> - Check for mail matching pattern

## Interface
```go
type AwaitHandler interface {
    Check(awaitID string) (completed bool, result string, err error)
}
```

 {"id":"gt-ngpz","title":"mol-christmas-launch: 3-day execution plan","description":"

---

## Wisp Integration Wave Plan (added 2025-12-21)

Dependency-ordered execution for gt-3x0z (Wisp Molecules) + gt-rana (Patrol System):

### Wave 1 (parallel - no blockers)
- gt-3x0z.1: gt rig init creates .beads-ephemeral/
- gt-3x0z.2: Configure bd for ephemeral molecule bonding
- gt-3x0z.3: gt doctor checks for ephemeral health
- gt-rana.1: Attachment field on pinned beads
- gt-rana.3: mol-deacon-patrol definition

### Wave 2 (after Wave 1)
- gt-3x0z.4: gt spawn --molecule bonds ephemeral (GATE 1)
- gt-rana.2: Daemon attachment detection

### Wave 3-5 (sequential)
- gt-3x0z.5, gt-3x0z.6 → gt-3x0z.7 → gt-3x0z.8 (GATE 2: squash)

### Wave 6+ (patrol integration)
- gt-rana.4: Basic patrol runner (needs gt-rana.3 + gt-3x0z.8)
- gt-3x0z.9: mol-deacon-patrol uses wisp (needs gt-rana.3 + gt-3x0z.8)
- Then: gt-rana.5-7, gt-3x0z.10-12

### Key Gates
1. gt-3x0z.4 - spawn/bond unlocks Phase 2
2. gt-3x0z.8 - squash unlocks patrol integration
3. gt-rana.4 - patrol runner unlocks Phase 2+ patrol

 {"id":"gt-oh90.1","title":"Polecat Arm (arm-toast)","description":"Single polecat inspection and action cycle.

This molecule is bonded dynamically by mol-witness-patrol's survey-workers step.
Each polecat being monitored gets one arm that runs in parallel with other arms.

## Variables

| Variable | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| polecat_name | Yes | Name of the polecat to inspect |
| rig | Yes | Rig containing the polecat |

## Step: capture
Capture recent tmux output for toast.

```bash
tmux capture-pane -t gt-gastown-toast -p | tail -50
```

Record:
- Last activity timestamp (when was last tool call?)
- Visible errors or stack traces
- Completion indicators ("Done", "Finished", etc.)

## Step: assess
Categorize polecat state based on captured output.

States:
- **working**: Recent tool calls, active processing
- **idle**: At prompt, no recent activity
- **error**: Showing errors or stack traces
- **requesting_shutdown**: Sent LIFECYCLE/Shutdown mail
- **done**: Showing completion indicators

Calculate: minutes since last activity.
Needs: capture

## Step: load-history
Read nudge history for toast from patrol state.

```
nudge_count = state.nudges[toast].count
last_nudge_time = state.nudges[toast].timestamp
```

This data was loaded by the parent patrol's load-state step and passed
to the arm via the bonding context.
Needs: assess

## Step: decide
Apply the nudge matrix to determine action for toast.

| State | Idle Time | Nudge Count | Action |
|-------|-----------|-------------|--------|
| working | any | any | none |
| idle | <10min | any | none |
| idle | 10-15min | 0 | nudge-1 (gentle) |
| idle | 15-20min | 1 | nudge-2 (direct) |
| idle | 20+min | 2 | nudge-3 (final) |
| idle | any | 3 | escalate |
| error | any | any | assess-severity |
| requesting_shutdown | any | any | pre-kill-verify |
| done | any | any | pre-kill-verify |

Nudge text:
1. "How's progress? Need any help?"
2. "Please wrap up soon. What's blocking you?"
3. "Final check. Will escalate in 5 min if no response."

Record decision and rationale.
Needs: load-history

## Step: execute
Take the decided action for toast.

**nudge-N**:
```bash
tmux send-keys -t gt-gastown-toast "{{nudge_text}}" Enter
```

**pre-kill-verify**:
```bash
cd polecats/toast
git status                    # Must be clean
git log origin/main..HEAD     # Check for unpushed
bd show <assigned-issue>      # Verify closed/deferred
```
If clean: kill session, remove worktree, delete branch
If dirty: record failure, retry next cycle

**escalate**:
```bash
gt mail send mayor/ -s "Escalation: toast stuck" -m "..."
```

**none**: No action needed.

Record: action taken, result, updated nudge count.
Needs: decide

## Output

The arm completes with:
- action_taken: none | nudge-1 | nudge-2 | nudge-3 | killed | escalated
- result: success | failed | pending
- updated_state: New nudge count and timestamp for toast

This data feeds back to the parent patrol's aggregate step.
---
bonded_from: mol-polecat-arm
bonded_to: gt-oh90
bonded_ref: arm-toast
bonded_at: 2025-12-23T10:00:00Z

 {"id":"gt-oiv0","title":"Remove bd sync instruction from polecat startup workflow","description":"Polecats are instructed to run `bd sync --from-main` on startup (spawn.go:634).

## Problem
- Spawn command already syncs beads before spawning (line 239)
- Polecats share rig-level beads via `.beads/redirect`
- Multiple polecats starting simultaneously all try to sync same shared beads
- This causes git conflicts/failures when many polecats spawn at once

## Observed
User reported: 'all polecats failing on beads sync on startup in one run'

## Fix
Remove line 634 from buildWorkAssignmentMail():
```
body.WriteString("2. Run \`bd sync --from-main\` to get fresh beads\n")
```

Polecats only need to sync at END of work (already in steps 5/7).

## Files
- internal/cmd/spawn.go: buildWorkAssignmentMail() and buildSpawnContext()","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:52.25177-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:21:03.03152-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:21:03.03152-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main"}

```bash
gt mol status [target]
```

Output:

- Current phase and progress
- Whether it's a wisp
- Next action hint

+{"id":"gt-v1pcg","title":"ApplyBranches/ApplyGates mutate steps in place","description":"ApplyBranches and ApplyGates claim to return a modified steps slice, but they actually mutate the original steps via the pointer map from buildStepMap().

```go
// ApplyBranches docstring says:
// Returns the modified steps slice (steps are modified in place for dependencies).
```

This is technically documented but violates the pattern established by ApplyLoops and ApplyAdvice which return new step slices.

Options:
1. Document explicitly that these functions mutate in place (current behavior)
2. Clone steps before modification for true immutability
3. Accept this as intentional since it's an optimization

The current behavior is safe because cook.go doesn't reuse the input slice, but could cause subtle bugs if callers expect immutability.

Recommend option 1 (document) or 2 (clone) for consistency.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:14:01.921527-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T15:14:01.921527-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-v1pcg","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:14:18.94711-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-zivp","title":"mol-outpost-assign: Intelligent work routing to outposts","description":"The federation design shows outposts.yaml with a static policy section. Simple preference ordering is fine as config, but intelligent work routing should be a molecule.

Current static approach:
```yaml
policy:
  default_preference: [local, gce-burst, cloudrun-burst]
```

Cases requiring cognition:
- "This is a long-running research task, route to VM not CloudRun"
- "This touches sensitive code, keep local"
- "These 5 issues are related, batch them to same outpost"
- "CloudRun cost is high today, prefer local even if slower"

## Molecule: outpost-assign
Intelligent work-to-outpost routing.

## Step: classify-work
Analyze the issue/work item:
- Expected duration (quick fix vs multi-hour)
- Resource requirements
- Sensitivity/security tier
- Related work (same epic?)

## Step: check-capacity
Query outpost status:
- Current load on each
- Cost accrued today
- Health status

## Step: select-outpost
Choose optimal outpost based on:
- Work classification
- Capacity/cost
- Policy constraints
Needs: classify-work, check-capacity

## Step: emit-assignment
Record decision in beads for audit.
Needs: select-outpost

## Notes
- This molecule is invoked by Mayor/Witness when spawning
- Simple cases can short-circuit to static policy
- Full analysis only for ambiguous cases","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:17.964834-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:17.964834-08:00"}

- Lines 116-143: Beads Configuration for Multi-Agent

This is a PGT implementation gap, not a design issue. +{"id":"gt-zn35j","title":"Nested loop support in control flow","description":"Currently, nested loops (a loop inside a loop body) are not supported.

In expandLoopIteration(), the Loop field is intentionally not copied:
```go
// Note: Loop field intentionally not copied - nested loops need explicit support
```

To support nested loops:
1. After expanding the outer loop iteration, recursively call ApplyLoops on the body steps
2. Handle nested iteration ID prefixing (e.g., `outer.iter1.inner.iter2.step`)
3. Add tests for nested loop scenarios

This is a lower-priority enhancement since most real-world formulas use single-level loops.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:13:49.880102-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T15:13:49.880102-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-zn35j","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:14:18.859367-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-zn9m","title":"142 instances of error suppression with _ = pattern","description":"Code has 142 instances of '_ = ' error suppression in non-test code.
Examples:

- internal/daemon/notification.go: _ = os.WriteFile() - ignores file write
- internal/mail/router.go: _ = r.notifyRecipient() - notification ignored

These create silent failures and hard-to-debug issues.

Options:
1. Document intentional suppressions with comments
2. Handle errors properly where appropriate
3. At minimum, log suppressed errors at debug level","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T12:50:59.241303-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T12:50:59.241303-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-zn9m","depends_on_id":"gt-jo9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T12:52:07.478225-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-zniu","title":"gt park command for parking molecules on external deps","description":"Add `gt park` command for when polecat hits external dependency:

```bash
gt park --step=gt-mol.3 --waiting="beads:mol-run-assignee"
```

This command:
1. Adds blocked_by: external:beads:mol-run-assignee to the step
2. Clears assignee on the step
3. Clears assignee on molecule root
4. Sends handoff mail to self with context
5. Shuts down polecat session

The molecule enters "parked" state (derived: in_progress + no assignee + blocked step).

Part of cross-project dependency system.
See: docs/cross-project-deps.md

Depends on Beads: bd-om4a (external: blocked_by support)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:39:01.401567-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T22:39:01.401567-08:00"}
